reasoning: Terminating the Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF) program requires an act of Congress (as it is a statutory program created in 2007) or a lengthy rulemaking process to halt new enrollments. The timeline from the May 14, 2025 knowledge cutoff to the July 1, 2025 resolution date is only about 45 days. While the 'Project 2025 Tracker' marked it as 'in progress' (likely in response to an early Trump administration proposal, budget blueprint, or executive order directing a review), finalizing the legislative repeal via a reconciliation bill or completing an Administrative Procedure Act (APA) rulemaking is highly unlikely to conclude before July 1. Reconciliation bills usually take until the late summer or fall, and APA rules take roughly 12-18 months. Therefore, it is highly improbable this objective will be marked 'complete' by July 1.
